messages.content: hi
Session start. Output mindmap, recent context, memory stats. Session active.
/mind-context full
Full mindmap rendered with all nodes including architecture, constraints, conventions at max depth.
/mind-context
Normal mode mindmap rendered with depth-filtered nodes.

Demande d'analyser les méthodologies du repo packetqc/knowledge pour identifier lesquelles importer. Veut un viewer JS statique unique pour documentation normalisée. Aussi rechercher une lib JS de mindmap interactive avec drag-and-drop de nodes.

Analyse complète du repo production: 6 méthodologies web identifiées, 2 layouts HTML (publication.html 109KB, default.html 74KB). Verdict: importer le CSS 4-thèmes et le print/export, adapter les layouts en viewer JS statique (pas Jekyll). Recherche libs mindmap: MindElixir (MIT, drag natif), Markmap (MIT, markdown input), jsMind, GoJS, vis-network, D3. Recommandation: MindElixir pour drag interactif.
Clarification: Jekyll/Liquid/kramdown est OK pour la production. Il faut adapter au nouveau système MAIS respecter ce qui est importé de production pour que le retour en production fonctionne.

Compris: contrainte bidirectionnelle. Le format reste compatible Jekyll (front matter + markdown). Le viewer JS statique est un ajout local pour K_DOCS, pas un remplacement. Contenu doit fonctionner dans les deux directions: production → K_DOCS (import) et K_DOCS → production (retour).

Approuve le plan. Demande de prendre la publication Knowledge 2.0 comme pilote (3 versions: source, summary, full). Les outils de pagination, navigation et cache client doivent rester fonctionnels. Pas nécessairement Jekyll compatible mais structure URL /docs/publications/<slug>/ doit correspondre à production.

Étape 1 exécutée: docs/ créé, install.sh mis à jour, mindmap enrichi avec 5 nodes documentation + work en cours, documentation.json peuplé avec 6 références production (3 méthodologies web, 2 layouts HTML, 1 publication pilote) et conventions documentaires.

tools.action: add documentation nodes + work en cours

messages.content: docs/ doit rester à la racine pour GitHub Pages. Utiliser Knowledge/K_DOCS pour implémentation du module, pas K_MIND. L'utilisateur voit le viewer sur le serveur local et trouve ça intéressant.

Créé Knowledge/K_DOCS/ comme module séparé. docs/ reste à la racine pour GitHub Pages. Viewer index.html créé avec 4 thèmes, Mermaid, TOC, export PDF. Serveur local lancé port 8000. Publication pilote Knowledge 2.0 (3 versions) téléchargée et servie.
Manque l'export DOCX, le format Letter/Legal, et le cover page PDF.

Ajouté: 1) DOCX export avec cover page + Mermaid SVG→PNG + page breaks. 2) Sélection Letter/Legal dans toolbar. 3) Cover page PDF avec titre/desc/meta/date, running footers, @page :first clean. 4) Smart TOC page break. 5) Toast notification.

tools.action: add DOCX export, Letter/Legal, cover page, printAs()

messages.content: go ! (continuing from compaction — add document header block)

Added document header block to docs/index.html — styled card (like TOC) showing: title + version badge, tagline, pub_meta + source link, generated timestamp. Hidden in print (cover page has this info). CSS class .doc-header with .doc-header-title, .doc-header-tagline, .doc-header-badge, .doc-header-meta, .doc-header-gen.

forgot to mention in specs. we need bring back the capability to switch FR and EN languages. can you put it top right, next to DOCX export button? ... two beautiful buttons EN and FR, the one active is toggle on the other off...

Added EN/FR language switch to toolbar — two joined buttons (EN|FR) with active/inactive states. Architecture: DOCS registry has path_fr field, FR files live under docs/fr/ prefix mirroring EN paths. Toggle loads paired version, updates URL with lang=fr param. FR disabled when no path_fr exists. Checking production repo for existing FR content.

tools.action: add lang-switch CSS, buttons, and toggle logic

messages.content: in fr version, toc is twice like same issue we had in en version. also, title is english in the first TOC that we want to keep but it should be french.

Fixed: 1) Manual TOC regex now strips both **Contents** and **Table des matières**. 2) Generated TOC title localized based on currentMeta.lang — 'Table des matières' in FR, 'Contents' in EN. 3) Generated label localized — 'Généré' in FR.

tools.action: update TOC strip regex for FR, localize TOC title and Generated label

messages.content: can we support the 4 actual interface web page type in this new viewer? all, same as concepts we bring from. should we import all documentation from knowledge repo to test it all.

Identified 3 layouts (default hub, publication, interface variant) across 77 EN + 76 FR files. Launched parallel agents to download all content from production repo. Production has 26 publications, 4 interfaces, 3 profile pages, 8 hub pages, 7 success stories.
will we are running this new session K_DOCS that have inherit from K_MIND, are you under the impression that K_MIND capabilities are running in realtime?
Acknowledged: memory_append has been inconsistent — dropped during fast-paced work. Catching up on missed turns now. Will maintain discipline going forward.
should we import all documentation from knowledge repo to test it all

Downloaded all 78 EN + 76 FR markdown files from production. Built full DOCS registry with 77 entries organized in 6 optgroups: Hub (8), Interfaces (4), Profile (4), Publications (52), Success Stories (9). Dropdown uses optgroup for clean navigation.
can we have this same kind of bar at bottom of the page? i would move the page layout and format and export capabilities in this bottom bar.

Split toolbar into two sticky bars: top bar for navigation (doc selector, theme, EN/FR), bottom bar for export (orientation, page size, PDF, DOCX). Bottom bar sticks to viewport bottom. Both hidden in print.

tools.action: split toolbar HTML into top/bottom, add toolbar-bottom CSS

messages.content: si tu regarde la page all links par le viewer, tu vas voir des liens brisés: il faut fixer ca. il y avait peut-être des icons à considérer...

Added Liquid template resolver to viewer. Handles: {{ path | relative_url }}, {{ site.baseurl }}, {{ site.url }}, {{ site.github_repo }}, {{ page.X }}, {% include %}. Covers 800+ Liquid tag occurrences across 40+ files. SITE config: url=packetqc.github.io, baseurl=/knowledge, github_repo=packetqc/knowledge.
we can adapt actual imported documentations to fit with the new viewer. it will become the production for documentation.

Added click interceptor for internal links: resolveInternalPath() maps /knowledge/... URLs to DOCS entries, interceptInternalLinks() catches clicks in #content and routes to loadWithLang(). Handles EN/FR auto-detection from path prefix. External and anchor links pass through.

tools.action: add resolveInternalPath + interceptInternalLinks functions, hook into loadDocument

messages.content: /mind-context then create child node under mindmap top level documentation called web docs and bring actual under it. then /mind-depth
Displayed mindmap context. Edited mind_memory.md: created web docs parent node under documentation, moved 5 existing children under it. Displayed depth config table.
/mind-depth conventions 3 then /mind-context
Set conventions depth to 3. Displayed updated depth config table and full mindmap context with web docs node visible under documentation.
we are working on nothing ?

User pointed out work category shows (none) in near memory. This is because mind_refs weren't tagging work nodes. Active work: docs/index.html viewer with click interceptor for internal links just added, Liquid resolver, bilingual support, export pipeline. Correcting tracking.
/mind-context — work still shows none

Found bug: mind_refs used knowledge:: prefix but categorization expects refs starting with top-level group name (work, documentation, conventions). Fixed all near_memory refs by stripping knowledge:: prefix.
why i do not see the skills when typing / ?
Only github skill had user_invocable: true. Added it to mind-context, mind-depth, mind-stats SKILL.md frontmatter. Skills now appear in / autocomplete.
we need to work in the interfaces now. let's start with the main interface.

Implemented interface page type support in the viewer. For page_type: interface docs (like Main Navigator), the viewer: 1) Detects {::nomarkdown} blocks in markdown, 2) Extracts raw HTML/CSS/JS, 3) Resolves Liquid templates, 4) Builds standalone HTML page via Blob URL, 5) Shows doc header + Launch button + iframe preview in viewer. Added embed mode (?embed param) that hides viewer toolbars for iframe loading. Rewrites production /knowledge/ URLs to viewer embed URLs for iframes and widget links.

tools.action: add embed mode, renderInterface(), URL rewriting for interfaces

messages.content: viewer is broken
Fixed: </script> literal inside JS string broke HTML parser. Replaced with string concatenation '</' + 'script>'. Viewer should work again.
Interface pages: no launch button, collapsible chrome bar with metadata at top, no EN/FR in chrome bar (viewer has it), interfaces always landscape by default

Rewrote renderInterface: no launch button, interface renders directly. Added collapsible chrome bar (title, version badge, description, pub_id, date, generated time) with localStorage persistence. Top toolbar stays visible (doc selector + lang). Bottom export toolbar hidden. Auto-landscape on interface load. exitInterfaceMode restores normal mode. CSS: iface-chrome bar styles, #content flex column fills viewport minus toolbar height.

tools.action: rewrite renderInterface with chrome bar, add CSS, auto-landscape, exitInterfaceMode restore

messages.content: layout capabilities not required in bar of interface page content. interfaces are always landscape by default. no EN/FR in chrome bar.

Stripped from main-navigator/index.md: language toggle, orientation toggle, panel selector, export toolbar override, theme propagation JS, and related CSS. Chrome bar now only has collapse toggle. All layout/export/lang handled by viewer. Also removed ~170 lines of production-specific JS.

tools.action: remove lang toggle, orient toggle, export override, theme propagation from chrome bar

messages.content: the is a collapsible that is not required anymore in the main interface: the last one

Removed main navigator's own chrome bar: HTML div, CSS (.chrome-bar, .chrome-collapsed), JS (applyChromeState, CHROME_KEY), print rule reference. Viewer's interface chrome bar is now the only collapsible.
perfect,
User confirmed interface chrome bar working. Awaiting next direction.
Left panel links must be routed (center vs right panel). Main Navigator link should reload interface. Getting about:blank.

Fixed: URLs were relative (index.html?doc=...) which can't resolve from Blob URL context. Changed to absolute URLs using viewerOrigin. _top target links (like I2 Main Navigator) get no &embed so they reload the full viewer. Center/right iframe links get &embed for content-only rendering.

tools.action: absolute URLs in rewriteUrl/viewerRewriteUrl, _top target handling without embed

messages.content: nothing gets loaded on the main interface right panel on click from the left panel links

Changed approach: instead of rewriting href attributes (which fail from Blob URL context), added click interceptor on nav panel that catches clicks, rewrites URLs, and sets iframe.src directly. _top targets use window.top.location.href.
[compaction resume — continuation of interface widget links fix]

Replaced Blob URL approach with document.write() in renderInterface(). The iframe now runs at same origin, enabling widget links in left panel to navigate center-frame and content-frame iframes. Changed: removed blob/blobUrl creation, iframe src attr removed, added ifaceFrame.contentDocument.open()/write(fullPage)/close() after DOM insertion.

tools.action: replace Blob URL with document.write() in renderInterface()

messages.content: not working

Found root cause: resolveLiquid() regex only matched single-quoted Liquid tags but navigator uses double quotes. {{ "" | relative_url }} was never resolved, so BASE stayed literal and all widget URLs were broken. Fixed regex to accept both quote styles. Also switched from document.write() to srcdoc for iframe.

tools.action: fix resolveLiquid regex for double-quoted Liquid tags
switch from document.write to srcdoc for interface iframe

messages.content: there was routing rules on the right panel when it is time to route an interface link: the issue was that the link of interface in a page on the right panel of the main interface will load the interface in the right panel instead of the center panel and there is a special route for this panel when it is the main interface link clicked inside of it to load at all page.

Added right panel routing rules in navigator IIFE: interface links in content-frame route to center-frame, main navigator link reloads full page via window.top.location.reload(). Interceptor re-attaches on each iframe load event.

tools.action: add right-panel click interceptor with routing rules

messages.content: interface links in right panel load in both center and right. only main works. srcdoc origin null prevents cross-doc access.

Moved right-panel routing from navigator (srcdoc, origin null) to viewer's interceptInternalLinks. When IS_EMBED && window.name === 'content-frame': interfaces route to parent center-frame, main-nav reloads top. Also soft-coupled SITE.baseurl to auto-detect from deployment path, with production_baseurl fallback for Liquid resolution.

tools.action: add right-panel routing in interceptInternalLinks + auto-detect baseurl
